Abstract: Molecular polymorphism of the ospC gene has been reported in Borrelia burgdorferi sensu stricto, Borrelia garinii and Borrelia afzelii, the spirochetes that cause Lyme disease. In this study, OspC sequences of five Borrelia valaisiana isolates were determined and compared with those of other Lyme disease spirochetes. The results indicate that OspC sequences of B. valaisiana are highly polymorphic and that some of them are more closely related to OspC sequences from B. garinii and B. afzelii than to other B. valaisiana OspC sequences. Phylogenetic analysis of OspC genes and of other genes indicates that the ospC gene is subject to frequent lateral gene transfer between different Borrelia species.
